title: Modern Go 1.26: Reliable Concurrent Systems
description: New programmers and working developers who need to build, test, secure, diagnose, and defend current Go programs from first execution through production-shaped concurrent services. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate go language specification through go 1.26, the go 1 compatibility model, current toolchain behavior, packages, modules, workspaces, testing, and standard-library design through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate cumulative runnable work across values, control flow, functions, collections, unicode, structs, methods, interfaces, errors, generics, goroutines, channels, context, synchronization, i/o, json, and service boundaries through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate production practice covering fuzzing, race evidence, security, vulnerability management, http lifecycles, diagnostics, profiling, pgo, release gates, and graceful operations through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

competencies.statement: Explain how source files, package declarations, imports, init order, and package main produce an executable program.
misconceptions: Every Go source file is an independently executable script.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain how source files, package declarations, imports, init order, and package main produce an executable program.

competencies.statement: Choose and interpret go run, build, install, test, fmt, vet, doc, env, version, and help evidence for a bounded task.
misconceptions: go run and go build produce the same durable artifact and evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose and interpret go run, build, install, test, fmt, vet, doc, env, version, and help evidence for a bounded task.

competencies.statement: Apply UTF-8 source, identifiers, keywords, tokens, semicolon insertion, comments, and formatting rules without relying on visual coincidence.
misconceptions: A newline can be inserted anywhere because gofmt always repairs it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply UTF-8 source, identifiers, keywords, tokens, semicolon insertion, comments, and formatting rules without relying on visual coincidence.

competencies.statement: Separate language version, toolchain version, runtime behavior, GOOS and GOARCH target, and browser-interpreter limitations.
misconceptions: Code accepted by one interpreter proves compatibility with every current Go toolchain and target.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate language version, toolchain version, runtime behavior, GOOS and GOARCH target, and browser-interpreter limitations.

competencies.statement: Predict output, run the smallest program, inspect diagnostics, change one input, and preserve a regression assertion.
misconceptions: One successful hello-world run demonstrates programming mastery.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict output, run the smallest program, inspect diagnostics, change one input, and preserve a regression assertion.

competencies.statement: Use booleans, strings, integers, floating-point, complex values, bytes, runes, and their zero values with explicit domain meaning.
misconceptions: A zero value is always invalid and should be replaced immediately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use booleans, strings, integers, floating-point, complex values, bytes, runes, and their zero values with explicit domain meaning.

competencies.statement: Choose var, short declaration, assignment, multiple assignment, and lexical scope while detecting shadowed state.
misconceptions: The short declaration operator always creates every name on its left side.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose var, short declaration, assignment, multiple assignment, and lexical scope while detecting shadowed state.

competencies.statement: Use typed and untyped constants, iota, representability, and explicit conversion without hiding unit or overflow risk.
misconceptions: Untyped constants use one fixed machine representation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use typed and untyped constants, iota, representability, and explicit conversion without hiding unit or overflow risk.

competencies.statement: Distinguish defined types, aliases, underlying types, assignability, conversion, and type identity.
misconceptions: Two defined types with the same underlying representation are freely assignable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish defined types, aliases, underlying types, assignability, conversion, and type identity.

competencies.statement: Use addresses, dereferencing, pointer parameters, escape-aware ownership, and Go 1.26 new(expression) optionals deliberately.
misconceptions: A pointer automatically provides shared mutable ownership and nil safety.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use addresses, dereferencing, pointer parameters, escape-aware ownership, and Go 1.26 new(expression) optionals deliberately.

competencies.statement: Trace arithmetic, comparison, logical, bitwise, shift, precedence, conversion, and evaluation-order guarantees from concrete values.
misconceptions: Operands and function arguments always evaluate strictly left to right.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace arithmetic, comparison, logical, bitwise, shift, precedence, conversion, and evaluation-order guarantees from concrete values.

competencies.statement: Use if statements with optional initializers and narrow variable scope around the decision they support.
misconceptions: A variable declared in an if initializer remains available after the if statement.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use if statements with optional initializers and narrow variable scope around the decision they support.

competencies.statement: Build bounded for loops and range loops while reasoning about copied iteration values, indexes, order, and Go 1.22 loop variables.
misconceptions: Range always yields references to stable collection elements in a deterministic order.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build bounded for loops and range loops while reasoning about copied iteration values, indexes, order, and Go 1.22 loop variables.

competencies.statement: Choose expression switches, type switches, labeled control, and select only when their matching and termination behavior is explicit.
misconceptions: Go switch cases fall through by default like C switch cases.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose expression switches, type switches, labeled control, and select only when their matching and termination behavior is explicit.

competencies.statement: Predict deferred-call argument capture, last-in-first-out execution, named-result interaction, and cleanup timing.
misconceptions: A deferred call evaluates all arguments only when the surrounding function returns.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict deferred-call argument capture, last-in-first-out execution, named-result interaction, and cleanup timing.

competencies.statement: Design function signatures, named types, parameters, results, and first-class function values around one responsibility.
misconceptions: Parameter names participate in function type identity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design function signatures, named types, parameters, results, and first-class function values around one responsibility.

competencies.statement: Use multiple results and the blank identifier without discarding failure or evidence that the caller needs.
misconceptions: Ignoring a returned error is harmless when another result looks usable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use multiple results and the blank identifier without discarding failure or evidence that the caller needs.

competencies.statement: Define and call variadic functions, distinguish the slice parameter, and forward existing slices with explicit ownership.
misconceptions: Passing a slice to a variadic function never aliases its backing array.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define and call variadic functions, distinguish the slice parameter, and forward existing slices with explicit ownership.

competencies.statement: Build closures while tracing captured variables, independent state, loop capture, lifetime, and concurrency risk.
misconceptions: Each closure automatically receives a frozen copy of every captured value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build closures while tracing captured variables, independent state, loop capture, lifetime, and concurrency risk.

competencies.statement: Use recursion only with a demonstrable base case, progress measure, depth bound, and iterative comparison.
misconceptions: A logically correct recursive definition cannot exhaust stack or time.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use recursion only with a demonstrable base case, progress measure, depth bound, and iterative comparison.

competencies.statement: Use arrays as fixed-length value types and explain how length participates in type identity and copying.
misconceptions: Arrays and slices have the same assignment and parameter semantics.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use arrays as fixed-length value types and explain how length participates in type identity and copying.

competencies.statement: Reason about slice length, capacity, backing storage, nil slices, empty non-nil slices, and observable API differences.
misconceptions: Every zero-length slice is nil and serializes identically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reason about slice length, capacity, backing storage, nil slices, empty non-nil slices, and observable API differences.

competencies.statement: Use two-index and full slice expressions while proving bounds, capacity, and append authority.
misconceptions: A subslice owns only its visible elements and cannot affect hidden capacity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use two-index and full slice expressions while proving bounds, capacity, and append authority.

competencies.statement: Predict whether append reuses or replaces storage and use copy or clone patterns to establish ownership.
misconceptions: append always allocates a new backing array.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict whether append reuses or replaces storage and use copy or clone patterns to establish ownership.

competencies.statement: Diagnose accidental large-array retention and evaluate preallocation from measured size and growth evidence.
misconceptions: Preallocating the largest imaginable capacity is always the fastest and cheapest choice.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose accidental large-array retention and evaluate preallocation from measured size and growth evidence.

competencies.statement: Create, update, delete, clear, and query maps while distinguishing missing keys from stored zero values.
misconceptions: A map lookup alone always reveals whether the key existed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create, update, delete, clear, and query maps while distinguishing missing keys from stored zero values.

competencies.statement: Choose comparable key types and explain map reference behavior, iteration instability, mutation rules, and concurrency limits.
misconceptions: Maps are safe for simultaneous reads and writes because they are reference-like.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose comparable key types and explain map reference behavior, iteration instability, mutation rules, and concurrency limits.

competencies.statement: Treat strings as immutable byte sequences and convert to or from byte slices with explicit allocation and encoding intent.
misconceptions: String length reports the number of user-perceived characters.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat strings as immutable byte sequences and convert to or from byte slices with explicit allocation and encoding intent.

competencies.statement: Decode UTF-8 with range and unicode/utf8 while handling byte indexes, replacement runes, invalid input, and grapheme limits.
misconceptions: One rune always equals one visible character.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Decode UTF-8 with range and unicode/utf8 while handling byte indexes, replacement runes, invalid input, and grapheme limits.

competencies.statement: Produce stable map-derived output by extracting and sorting keys under a documented comparison rule.
misconceptions: Repeated map iteration in one process guarantees the same order.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Produce stable map-derived output by extracting and sorting keys under a documented comparison rule.

competencies.statement: Design structs, field visibility, literals, zero states, comparison, and tags as explicit data contracts.
misconceptions: Struct tags enforce validation automatically at compile time.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design structs, field visibility, literals, zero states, comparison, and tags as explicit data contracts.

competencies.statement: Choose value or pointer receivers from mutation, copying, nil behavior, consistency, and method-set evidence.
misconceptions: Pointer receivers are always faster and therefore always correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose value or pointer receivers from mutation, copying, nil behavior, consistency, and method-set evidence.

competencies.statement: Predict method sets, implicit address-taking, interface satisfaction, and behavior of named pointer and value types.
misconceptions: A value and pointer to that value always satisfy exactly the same interfaces.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict method sets, implicit address-taking, interface satisfaction, and behavior of named pointer and value types.

competencies.statement: Use embedding for promotion only after resolving name collisions, initialization, API exposure, and substitution limits.
misconceptions: Embedding creates inheritance and makes the outer type a subtype of the embedded type.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use embedding for promotion only after resolving name collisions, initialization, API exposure, and substitution limits.

competencies.statement: Compose small types and constructors so invalid state, ownership, and transitions remain observable and testable.
misconceptions: A constructor can guarantee invariants even when callers may still use a public zero-value struct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compose small types and constructors so invalid state, ownership, and transitions remain observable and testable.

competencies.statement: Define small consumer-owned interfaces and use implicit satisfaction without premature abstraction.
misconceptions: Types must explicitly declare that they implement an interface.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define small consumer-owned interfaces and use implicit satisfaction without premature abstraction.

competencies.statement: Trace interface dynamic type and value, nil interfaces, typed nil pointers, comparison, and method dispatch.
misconceptions: An interface containing a typed nil pointer compares equal to nil.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace interface dynamic type and value, nil interfaces, typed nil pointers, comparison, and method dispatch.

competencies.statement: Use checked type assertions and type switches only where dynamic behavior is part of the boundary contract.
misconceptions: A failed one-result type assertion returns the target type zero value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use checked type assertions and type switches only where dynamic behavior is part of the boundary contract.

competencies.statement: Create, return, wrap, join, inspect, and classify error values with errors.Is and errors.As while preserving causes.
misconceptions: Matching errors by message text is the most stable classification method.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create, return, wrap, join, inspect, and classify error values with errors.Is and errors.As while preserving causes.

competencies.statement: Place interfaces at consumer boundaries, return concrete useful types, and evaluate mocks, adapters, and evolution cost.
misconceptions: Every exported concrete type should immediately receive a matching interface.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Place interfaces at consumer boundaries, return concrete useful types, and evaluate mocks, adapters, and evolution cost.

competencies.statement: Design error contracts that state operation, relevant identity, cause, retry meaning, and partial-result policy.
misconceptions: Every error should be logged and wrapped at every call layer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design error contracts that state operation, relevant identity, cause, retry meaning, and partial-result policy.

competencies.statement: Acquire and release resources in the owning scope, check close or flush failures when relevant, and avoid loop-defer retention.
misconceptions: defer inside a long loop releases each resource at the end of its iteration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Acquire and release resources in the owning scope, check close or flush failures when relevant, and avoid loop-defer retention.

competencies.statement: Reserve panic for unrecoverable invariants or initialization failures and keep expected input failures as errors.
misconceptions: Panic is Go exception handling and should replace ordinary error returns.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reserve panic for unrecoverable invariants or initialization failures and keep expected input failures as errors.

competencies.statement: Recover only in the same goroutine at a deliberate isolation boundary, restore invariants, and convert or report the failure honestly.
misconceptions: A recover call anywhere in the process catches panics from every goroutine.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Recover only in the same goroutine at a deliberate isolation boundary, restore invariants, and convert or report the failure honestly.

competencies.statement: Reproduce the smallest failure, identify its causal layer, repair it, and retain a test or invariant over changed input.
misconceptions: Removing the observed error message proves the underlying defect is fixed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reproduce the smallest failure, identify its causal layer, repair it, and retain a test or invariant over changed input.

competencies.statement: Organize commands, internal packages, public packages, names, imports, cycles, and documentation around cohesive APIs.
misconceptions: A package named util is a good universal home for unrelated helpers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Organize commands, internal packages, public packages, names, imports, cycles, and documentation around cohesive APIs.

competencies.statement: Interpret module, go, toolchain, require, replace, exclude, retract, and godebug directives without treating local overrides as releases.
misconceptions: A replace directive in a consumer module publishes the replacement for all downstream users.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret module, go, toolchain, require, replace, exclude, retract, and godebug directives without treating local overrides as releases.

competencies.statement: Explain semantic import versioning, minimal version selection, module graphs, sums, proxies, and private-module boundaries.
misconceptions: Minimal version selection always chooses the newest version available on the internet.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain semantic import versioning, minimal version selection, module graphs, sums, proxies, and private-module boundaries.

competencies.statement: Use go.work for local multi-module development while proving builds remain correct outside the workspace.
misconceptions: A committed go.work file is always required for a multi-module repository.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use go.work for local multi-module development while proving builds remain correct outside the workspace.

competencies.statement: Review direct and transitive dependency additions, version upgrades, licenses, checksums, vulnerability evidence, and rollback.
misconceptions: A green go mod tidy proves a dependency is trustworthy and necessary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Review direct and transitive dependency additions, version upgrades, licenses, checksums, vulnerability evidence, and rollback.

competencies.statement: Define generic functions and types, follow type inference, and retain relationships among parameters and results.
misconceptions: Generics erase all static type relationships at the call site.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define generic functions and types, follow type inference, and retain relationships among parameters and results.

competencies.statement: Build minimal constraints from method elements, unions, underlying-type approximations, comparable, and intersection semantics.
misconceptions: A constraint interface can always be used as an ordinary runtime value type.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build minimal constraints from method elements, unions, underlying-type approximations, comparable, and intersection semantics.

competencies.statement: Use only operations guaranteed by the type set and explain why fields, methods, conversions, and nil differ across type arguments.
misconceptions: Any operation valid for one member of a type set is valid in generic code.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use only operations guaranteed by the type set and explain why fields, methods, conversions, and nil differ across type arguments.

competencies.statement: Compare generics, interfaces, concrete functions, code generation, and reflection from relationship, performance, and API needs.
misconceptions: Replacing every interface with a type parameter makes code more reusable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare generics, interfaces, concrete functions, code generation, and reflection from relationship, performance, and API needs.

competencies.statement: Use range-over-function iteration and evaluate Go 1.26 self-referential constraints while preserving cancellation and readability.
misconceptions: A current language feature is automatically supported by every older toolchain and embedded interpreter.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use range-over-function iteration and evaluate Go 1.26 self-referential constraints while preserving cancellation and readability.

competencies.statement: Write table-driven tests and subtests whose names, fixtures, assertions, and changed cases expose the failed contract.
misconceptions: A table-driven test is automatically clearer than focused individual tests.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write table-driven tests and subtests whose names, fixtures, assertions, and changed cases expose the failed contract.

competencies.statement: Choose unit, integration, end-to-end, golden, example, and contract tests with narrow fakes at owned boundaries.
misconceptions: Mocking every collaborator produces the most realistic confidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose unit, integration, end-to-end, golden, example, and contract tests with narrow fakes at owned boundaries.

competencies.statement: Define fuzz properties, seed corpora, bounds, failure minimization, and retained regression inputs.
misconceptions: Fuzzing proves the absence of defects once it runs without failure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define fuzz properties, seed corpora, bounds, failure minimization, and retained regression inputs.

competencies.statement: Design benchmarks with controlled work and allocations, compare statistically, and interpret coverage as location evidence rather than quality.
misconceptions: A single benchmark number and high statement coverage prove production performance and correctness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design benchmarks with controlled work and allocations, compare statistically, and interpret coverage as location evidence rather than quality.

competencies.statement: Gate formatting, tests, vet analyzers, Go 1.26 modernizers, race checks, and relevant static analysis with explicit ownership.
misconceptions: go vet is a complete lint, security, and correctness proof.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Gate formatting, tests, vet analyzers, Go 1.26 modernizers, race checks, and relevant static analysis with explicit ownership.

competencies.statement: Start goroutines only with named ownership, inputs, outputs, stop conditions, panic boundary, and completion evidence.
misconceptions: Goroutines are free and automatically stop when their caller returns.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Start goroutines only with named ownership, inputs, outputs, stop conditions, panic boundary, and completion evidence.

competencies.statement: Use unbuffered, buffered, and directional channels while assigning close authority to the sender that knows no values remain.
misconceptions: A receiver should close a channel when it no longer wants values.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use unbuffered, buffered, and directional channels while assigning close authority to the sender that knows no values remain.

competencies.statement: Distinguish open, closed, empty, nil, and buffered channel states and interpret two-result receives and range termination.
misconceptions: Receiving from a closed channel blocks forever.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish open, closed, empty, nil, and buffered channel states and interpret two-result receives and range termination.

competencies.statement: Use select, nil-channel disabling, default, timers, and explicit deadlines without assuming deterministic ready-case choice.
misconceptions: select always chooses the first ready case in source order.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use select, nil-channel disabling, default, timers, and explicit deadlines without assuming deterministic ready-case choice.

competencies.statement: Build bounded fan-out and fan-in stages with one ownership model, ordered-result policy, error propagation, and leak checks.
misconceptions: Closing the final output channel is enough to stop every upstream stage.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build bounded fan-out and fan-in stages with one ownership model, ordered-result policy, error propagation, and leak checks.

competencies.statement: Pass context as the first operation parameter across call boundaries and avoid storing it as durable domain state.
misconceptions: A Context belongs in every struct so future methods can reuse it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pass context as the first operation parameter across call boundaries and avoid storing it as durable domain state.

competencies.statement: Derive, propagate, call, and observe cancellation, deadlines, timeouts, and causes while releasing timer resources.
misconceptions: Discarding a CancelFunc is harmless because timeout cleanup is immediate.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive, propagate, call, and observe cancellation, deadlines, timeouts, and causes while releasing timer resources.

competencies.statement: Use context values only for request-scoped cross-boundary metadata with collision-safe keys, not optional parameters.
misconceptions: Context values are a type-safe replacement for all function parameters.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use context values only for request-scoped cross-boundary metadata with collision-safe keys, not optional parameters.

competencies.statement: Bound queues and concurrency, choose block, reject, shed, or degrade behavior, and measure saturation.
misconceptions: A larger buffered channel permanently fixes overload.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound queues and concurrency, choose block, reject, shed, or degrade behavior, and measure saturation.

competencies.statement: Detect leaked goroutines with ownership traces, cancellation tests, profiles, and changed failure cases.
misconceptions: A stable process response proves no goroutines are leaking.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect leaked goroutines with ownership traces, cancellation tests, profiles, and changed failure cases.

competencies.statement: Identify conflicting memory operations and establish sequenced-before, synchronized-before, and happens-before evidence.
misconceptions: Plausible output means concurrent reads observed the latest writes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify conflicting memory operations and establish sequenced-before, synchronized-before, and happens-before evidence.

competencies.statement: Protect one documented invariant with Mutex or RWMutex while minimizing lock scope and avoiding copy or reentrancy errors.
misconceptions: RWMutex is always faster whenever reads outnumber writes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Protect one documented invariant with Mutex or RWMutex while minimizing lock scope and avoiding copy or reentrancy errors.

competencies.statement: Use Once, OnceValue, Cond, and channel alternatives only when their wakeup, predicate-loop, and failure semantics fit.
misconceptions: One Cond signal permanently records an event for future waiters.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Once, OnceValue, Cond, and channel alternatives only when their wakeup, predicate-loop, and failure semantics fit.

competencies.statement: Use typed atomics for isolated counters or immutable snapshot publication and reject multi-variable invariants without coordination.
misconceptions: Making each field atomic makes a compound state transition atomic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use typed atomics for isolated counters or immutable snapshot publication and reject multi-variable invariants without coordination.

competencies.statement: Run representative workloads with the race detector, interpret reports, retain reproductions, and state unexecuted-path limits.
misconceptions: A clean go test -race run proves every possible execution is race-free.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run representative workloads with the race detector, interpret reports, retain reproductions, and state unexecuted-path limits.

competencies.statement: Compose io.Reader and io.Writer boundaries, buffering, limited reads, copying, and streaming without assuming one read fills a buffer.
misconceptions: Reader.Read always returns either data or an error, never both.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compose io.Reader and io.Writer boundaries, buffering, limited reads, copying, and streaming without assuming one read fills a buffer.

competencies.statement: Open files with explicit ownership and permissions, handle paths safely, and design temp-write, sync, rename, and cleanup evidence.
misconceptions: Writing directly to the destination and calling Close guarantees an atomic durable update.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Open files with explicit ownership and permissions, handle paths safely, and design temp-write, sync, rename, and cleanup evidence.

competencies.statement: Encode and decode JSON with field visibility, tags, unknown-field policy, numbers, nulls, optional values, and streaming limits.
misconceptions: Successful json.Unmarshal proves every input field matched the intended schema.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Encode and decode JSON with field visibility, tags, unknown-field policy, numbers, nulls, optional values, and streaming limits.

competencies.statement: Use time values, durations, locations, monotonic comparisons, timers, tickers, parsing, and serialization with explicit clock assumptions.
misconceptions: A time.Duration stores a calendar-aware number of days and months.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use time values, durations, locations, monotonic comparisons, timers, tickers, parsing, and serialization with explicit clock assumptions.

competencies.statement: Design command flags, stdin/stdout/stderr, exit status, signal cancellation, help, and machine-readable output as an interface contract.
misconceptions: Printing an error to stdout and returning from main guarantees a nonzero exit status.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design command flags, stdin/stdout/stderr, exit status, signal cancellation, help, and machine-readable output as an interface contract.

competencies.statement: Design Handler and ServeMux boundaries, method and path semantics, request limits, response status, headers, and streaming order.
misconceptions: Writing a response body before WriteHeader still allows any later status code.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design Handler and ServeMux boundaries, method and path semantics, request limits, response status, headers, and streaming order.

competencies.statement: Reuse configured clients and transports, set request contexts and timeouts, close bodies, bound reads, and classify status separately from transport errors.
misconceptions: http.Client.Get returns an error for every non-2xx response.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reuse configured clients and transports, set request contexts and timeouts, close bodies, bound reads, and classify status separately from transport errors.

competencies.statement: Validate untrusted input, escape output for its sink, minimize authority and data, protect secrets, and set explicit server resource limits.
misconceptions: Using a memory-safe language removes injection, authorization, resource-exhaustion, and logic risks.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate untrusted input, escape output for its sink, minimize authority and data, protect secrets, and set explicit server resource limits.

competencies.statement: Map internal failures to stable external responses while retaining structured correlation, cause, latency, and privacy-safe logs.
misconceptions: Returning the full internal error text gives clients the most useful and secure diagnosis.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map internal failures to stable external responses while retaining structured correlation, cause, latency, and privacy-safe logs.

competencies.statement: Coordinate signal handling, readiness withdrawal, server shutdown, in-flight deadlines, background-worker completion, and forced-exit evidence.
misconceptions: Calling Server.Close immediately is equivalent to graceful Shutdown.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Coordinate signal handling, readiness withdrawal, server shutdown, in-flight deadlines, background-worker completion, and forced-exit evidence.

competencies.statement: Explain stack and heap escape, allocation, garbage-collection cost, Go 1.26 Green Tea behavior, and measurement limits without manual-memory myths.
misconceptions: Every pointer allocates on the heap and every allocation should be eliminated.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain stack and heap escape, allocation, garbage-collection cost, Go 1.26 Green Tea behavior, and measurement limits without manual-memory myths.

competencies.statement: Choose CPU, heap, block, mutex, goroutine, execution trace, metrics, logs, or debugger evidence for the observed question.
misconceptions: A CPU profile is the correct first tool for every latency or memory problem.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose CPU, heap, block, mutex, goroutine, execution trace, metrics, logs, or debugger evidence for the observed question.

competencies.statement: Benchmark a representative baseline, form a causal hypothesis, inspect profiles, change one factor, validate behavior, and evaluate PGO portability.
misconceptions: A microbenchmark improvement guarantees lower production latency.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Benchmark a representative baseline, form a causal hypothesis, inspect profiles, change one factor, validate behavior, and evaluate PGO portability.

competencies.statement: Gate current toolchain patches, tests, race and fuzz evidence, govulncheck reachability, dependency provenance, reproducible builds, and rollback.
misconceptions: A dependency advisory always proves the built binary is exploitable, while no advisory proves safety.
